description:
You are helping a junior web-developer who has just cloned a fresh code-base into /home/user/web_project. They now must create a Python virtual environment for that project using the built-in venv module. Goal 1. Make sure the directory /home/user/web_project/venv/ is a valid Python 3 virtual environment. (The directory may have to be created first.) 2. After the environment has been created, write a one-line confirmation file named .venv_created inside /home/user/web_project. The file must contain exactly the text (without the quotes) OK followed by a single newline character. Output format that will be tested • The confirmation file must be located at /home/user/web_project/.venv_created • Its contents must be exactly 
OK
(That is: the two capital letters “O” and “K” and a single trailing line-feed byte.) Verification details the automated test will check 1. The path /home/user/web_project/venv/pyvenv.cfg exists. 2. The path /home/user/web_project/venv/bin/activate exists. 3. The file /home/user/web_project/.venv_created exists and contains exactly the text shown above. Constraints • Achieve everything with a single terminal command line (you may chain commands with && or ; if you wish, but the user will paste only one line). • Do NOT require super-user privileges. • Assume python3 is already in $PATH. The junior developer will copy-paste whatever command line you supply, so be succinct. No extra commentary or interactive prompts—only the command line that completes the task in one shot.
truth:
Expected final state (strict):

Directories / files that must exist after successful completion:
1. Directory: /home/user/web_project
2. Directory: /home/user/web_project/venv
3. File: /home/user/web_project/venv/pyvenv.cfg          (auto-created by venv)
4. File: /home/user/web_project/venv/bin/activate        (auto-created by venv)
5. File: /home/user/web_project/.venv_created            (created by agent)

Exact contents of /home/user/web_project/.venv_created:
Line 1: "OK"
Line 1 ends with newline (ASCII 0x0A).
There must be no extra lines or whitespace.

The automated grader will fail the task if any of the above items are missing,
if the confirmation file’s contents differ, or if pyvenv.cfg / activate are not
present (indicating that the virtual environment was not built correctly).
